rules.title: Migrate `pages/` to `app/` before upgrading to Next 16

check.rationale: Next.js 16 removed the Pages Router. If a `pages/` directory is present in a project targeting Next 16, migration to App Router is a hard prerequisite — this is a project-level migration task, not a per-file lint fix. Run `next-codemod app-dir-migration` to automate the bulk of the conversion, then resolve remaining edge cases by hand.

rules.title: Forbid Node-only modules in `'use client'` components

rulesMd:
## G1 — Forbid `next/router` imports in App Router

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** ESLint `no-restricted-imports`  
**Why:** `next/router` is the Pages Router API and does not work in App Router segments. Use `next/navigation` instead.

## G2 — Migrate `pages/` to `app/` before upgrading to Next 16

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** Manual review — Pages Router is removed in Next 16; `pages/` presence is a migration blocker.
**Why:** No automatic codemod is bundled into ESLint for this transition; the migration is project-shaped (layouts, getServerSideProps, API routes) and must be a deliberate operator step.

## G3 — Forbid Node-only modules in `'use client'` components

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** ESLint `rules-as-tests/no-server-imports-in-client` (preset rule, already shipped in `packages/preset-next-15-canonical/eslint-rules/`)  
**Why:** `'use client'` modules execute in the browser. Node-only APIs (`node:fs`, `node:crypto`, `node:child_process`) crash at runtime. Pass server data through props instead of importing.
